Plaintiff'S Motion To Quash Subpoenas
Off Calendar May-05-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Timothy J. Kodani for plaintiff and moving party
Kathleen M. Rotow for defendant and opposing party
HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion to quash subpoenas is granted as to the subpoenas to Seton Medical Center, California Pacific Medical Center, and the University of California at San Francisco for records of Gennedy Kislyuk. The motion is denied as moot relating to the subpoenas to Kaiser Permanente as the Court previously ordered plaintiff to execute a release for said records or be precluded from offering any expert testimony or other evidence that he has suffered damages as described in his responses to interrogatories.

Plaintiff's motion to quash subpoenas is granted as to the subpoenas for records of Victoria Kislyuk. The Court notes that plaintiff's responses to interrogatories indicates that she has not sought treatment from any health care providers as a result of the conduct alleged in the complaint. Plaintiff Victoria Kislyuk is therefore precluded from offering any expert testimony that she has suffered damages as described in her responses to interrogatories. Additionally, the trier of fact shall be instructed that Victoria Kislyuk neither sought nor received treatment for the mental and emotional pain, suffering, and humiliation described in her responses to interrogatories. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Motion To Compel Independent Medical Examination; Order Authorizing Release Of Psychiatric Records & Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-22-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
See appearance in previous motion.
Ruling:
Argued: tentation ruling adopted: Defendant's motion for an independent mental examination and for release of records: Granted. The Court finds good cause to order a mental examination as it is undisputed that plaintiff has claimed continuing depression and post-traumatic stress syndrome and has not offered to enter into a stipulation pursuant to CCP Section 2032(d). Defendant previously attempted to make a reasonable and good faith attempt to arrange for the examination by agreement. A separate statement pursuant to California Rule of Court 335 is not required because plaintiff did not respond with a substantive objection to the basis of the request.
The Court finds good cause pursuant to CCP Section 2024 (e) and orders that plaintiff Gennedy Kislyuk submit to a mental examination by Dr. Daniel Chaffin, at Legalink Court Reporting, 601 Van Ness Avenue, Suite 2052, San Francisco, California 94102. The examination shall consist of a clinical interview not to exceed four hours. The report of said examination shall be provided to counsel for plaintiff by May 9, 2005 or as soon as it is completed, whichever occurs earlier. The parties may re-open the deposition of Dr. Chaffin for further examination as to any opinions formed after conducting the examination of plaintiff and the review of any medical records.
The Court further orders that plaintiff execute a release for records from the medical providers identified in plaintiff's responses to form interrogatories. The release shall be signed and personally delivered to counsel for defendant by 10:00 a.m. April 26, 2005. If plaintiff fails to execute said release, plaintiffs will be precluded from offering any expert testimony or other evidence that plaintiff Gennedy Kislyuk has suffered damages as described in his responses to interrogatories.
All requests for monetary sanctions are denied. Comm. Bruce E. Chan.
